name: Netlify
description: Pioneer of JAMstack hosting with a feature-rich platform for building, deploying, and scaling modern web projects from a global edge network

description.value: Pioneer of JAMstack hosting with a feature-rich platform for building, deploying, and scaling modern web projects from a global edge network

parent-company.comment: Privately held and independently operated. Founded by Mathias Biilmann and Christian Bach. Raised $105M Series D in Nov 2021 at $2B valuation.

year-launched.comment: Launched on April 7, 2015 (previously operated as BitBalloon). Founded in 2014.

open-source.comment: The core platform is proprietary. Netlify open-sources CLI tools, framework adapters, and created Decap CMS (formerly Netlify CMS), but the hosting platform itself is not open source.

primary-focus.comment: Originally pioneered the JAMstack concept. Has since expanded to full-stack with serverless functions, edge functions, Blobs storage, and database integrations.

free-tier.comment: Generous free tier with no credit card required. Sites are suspended if limits are exceeded in a billing month.

free-tier-bandwidth.comment: As of the credit-based pricing model (Sep 2025). Site is suspended for the remainder of the month if exceeded on the free plan.

free-tier-build-minutes.comment: Legacy free plan includes 300 build minutes. Credit-based plans use a credit system rather than explicit build minute quotas.

free-tier-sites.comment: Free, Personal, and Pro plans all allow up to 500 projects. Usage limits remain the same regardless of number of projects.

paid-plan-starting-price.comment: Pro plan at $19/month per team member. Includes 1 TB bandwidth, 25,000 build minutes, and 3 concurrent builds.

bandwidth-overage-cost.comment: Applies to Pro plan. On the free plan, sites are suspended rather than incurring overage charges.

enterprise-pricing.comment: Enterprise plan includes SSO/SCIM, audit logs, SOC 2, HIPAA compliance, 99.99% uptime SLA, and 24/7 dedicated support.

git-integration.comment: Supports GitHub, GitHub Enterprise Server, GitLab (including self-managed), Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps.

auto-deploy.comment: Pushes to a connected branch automatically trigger builds and deployments.

cli-deployment.comment: Netlify CLI supports manual deploys, local dev server, and other deployment workflows.

build-system.comment: Full CI/CD build pipeline with configurable build commands, environment variables, and build plugins.

supported-frameworks.comment: Auto-detects 26+ frameworks including Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby, Astro, Hugo, Jekyll, SvelteKit, Remix, Angular, Vue CLI, Eleventy, Vite, React Router, Hydrogen, Gridsome, Hexo, MkDocs, RedwoodJS, TanStack Start, and more.

monorepo-support.comment: Supports automatic monorepo detection and manual base/package directory configuration.

deploy-previews.comment: Automatically creates preview deployments with unique URLs for pull/merge requests.

instant-rollbacks.comment: Any previous deploy can be published as the live version without rebuilding. Deploys can also be locked to prevent new deploys from going live.

deploy-notifications.comment: Supports Slack, email, outgoing webhooks, and GitHub commit status/checks. Available on Pro and Enterprise plans.

global-cdn.comment: Every deploy is published across all global edge locations.

cdn-provider.comment: Netlify operates its own edge network running across multiple cloud providers including AWS, Google Cloud, and DigitalOcean for redundancy.

edge-locations.comment: Netlify Edge has 70+ points of presence worldwide.

http2.comment: HTTP/2 is supported when HTTPS is enabled. Includes request multiplexing and compressed headers, but server push was dropped.

http3.comment: HTTP/3 (QUIC) does not appear to be supported as of early 2026 based on community forum inquiries.

response-headers.comment: Custom response headers can be configured via _headers file or netlify.toml, with path-level granularity using wildcards.

image-optimization.comment: Netlify Image CDN provides on-demand image transformation (resize, crop, reformat) with automatic format negotiation and edge caching.

asset-compression.comment: Brotli compression is applied automatically at build time. JS files are ~14% smaller, HTML ~21% smaller, and CSS ~17% smaller than gzip equivalents.

custom-domains.comment: Custom domains can be attached to any site, with Netlify DNS available as an optional managed DNS service.

free-ssl.comment: Automatic SSL certificate provisioning via Let's Encrypt at no cost.

wildcard-ssl.comment: Wildcard SSL certificates are supported. Netlify can automatically generate wildcard DNS records and SSL certificates for deploy previews.

auto-https-redirect.comment: HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect is handled automatically when HTTPS is enabled.

custom-domain-preview.comment: Automatic deploy subdomains are available on Pro plans and above. Custom domains can be set for deploy previews and branch deploys.

serverless-functions.comment: Built-in serverless functions that run on-demand. Free plan includes 125,000 function invocations per month.

functions-runtime.comment: Serverless functions support Node.js (with TypeScript) and Go. Edge functions run on the Deno runtime (JavaScript/TypeScript).

edge-functions.comment: Edge functions run on the Deno runtime at edge locations closest to the user. Free plan includes 1 million edge function invocations per month.

scheduled-functions.comment: Scheduled functions use cron expression format and execute on UTC timezone.

form-handling.comment: Built-in form handling with automatic HTML form detection at deploy time. Includes spam filtering via Akismet, file uploads, and Zapier integration.

authentication.comment: Netlify Identity provides built-in user authentication with signups, logins, password recovery, user metadata, and roles.

kv-storage.comment: Netlify Blobs is a key-value/blob store optimized for frequent reads and infrequent writes, with configurable consistency model.

database-integration.comment: No first-party integrated database. Offers official integrations with Supabase and PlanetScale that streamline environment variable setup and connection management, but these are third-party services.

redirects.comment: Supports 301, 302, and other redirect status codes via _redirects file or netlify.toml configuration.

rewrites.comment: Rewrites (200 status redirects) and reverse proxying are supported. URL stays the same while content is fetched from a different path or external URL.

custom-404.comment: Adding a 404.html file to the publish directory will automatically serve it for non-existent paths.

spa-fallback.comment: SPA fallback is achieved via a rewrite rule (e.g., '/* /index.html 200') that serves index.html for all unmatched paths.

headers-config.comment: Custom response headers can be defined per-path or globally via _headers file or netlify.toml. Supports wildcards for path matching.

ab-testing.comment: Branch-based split testing divides traffic between deployed Git branches at the CDN level with configurable percentages. Also achievable via Edge Functions.

free-team-members.comment: Free plan does not allow additional team member seats. However, unlimited free Reviewer roles can be added for deploy preview collaboration.

rbac.comment: Includes Owner, Developer, and Reviewer roles with customizable site-level access. SCIM provisioning available on Enterprise.

audit-logs.comment: Available on Pro and Enterprise plans. Includes team-level and site-level event filtering.

environment-variables.comment: Environment variables can be configured per-site for builds and functions, with support for different deploy contexts.

preview-comments.comment: Netlify Drawer on deploy previews features a conversation pane where stakeholders can review and add comments that sync to the connected pull/merge request.

ddos-protection.comment: Automatic DDoS protection monitors traffic patterns, identifies and blocks malicious traffic, and rate limits connections.

waf.comment: Netlify WAF includes predefined security rules based on OWASP Top 10 and Netlify-specific network patterns. Available as an add-on.

soc2.comment: SOC 2 Type II certified with annual third-party auditing. Also holds ISO 27001 certification.

gdpr.comment: Compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and PCI-DSS.

password-protection.comment: Sites can be password-protected for production, specific paths, or preview environments.

ip-access-control.comment: Traffic rules can be configured to block or allow traffic from specific IP addresses or geographic locations.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive, well-organized documentation with tutorials, API references, framework-specific guides, and a searchable knowledge base. Regularly updated with new features.

integrations.comment: Extensive integration hub with 150+ integrations across all major categories including CMS (Contentful, Sanity, Storyblok), analytics (Datadog, Lighthouse), commerce (commercetools, BigCommerce), auth, database, and search.

community.comment: Active official support forum at answers.netlify.com. Strong Stack Overflow presence. No official Discord server (only community-run).

status-page.comment: Public status page at netlifystatus.com with real-time status, uptime history, incident history, and API access.
